title: Postpartum bone health: calcium, vitamin D, and movement
excerpt: Pregnancy and breastfeeding draw on maternal calcium stores — food, supplements, and gentle exercise support recovery.
content:
## Why it matters

The fetus and breast milk use maternal calcium; low reserves may contribute to lasting aches.

## Nutrition

- Pasteurized milk, yogurt, soft-bone fish, tofu, leafy greens.
- Vitamin D from food, brief sun exposure, or supplements if prescribed.

## Activity

Walking and approved exercises once cleared; avoid prolonged bed rest.

## Testing

Bone density or labs only when your clinician recommends — do not self-diagnose osteoporosis.
